text.content:
2
*�����������������
# me - this DAT
# 
# comp - the replicator component which is cooking
# allOps - a list of all replicants, created or existing
# newOps - the subset that were just created
# template - table DAT specifying the replicator attributes
# master - the master operator
#

def replicate(comp, allOps, newOps, template, master):

	for c in newOps:
		#c.display = True
		#c.render = True
		#c.par.display = 1
		c.op('selVolume').par.channames = 'Volume' + str(c.digits)
		c.outputConnectors[0].connect(op('math1'))
		pass

	return

2
*�����������������The MultiMix component - A component equivalent to a Swiss Army Knife.

It can be used as a mixer (video and audio) a pixel mapper and a general compositing tool to use to control each layers opacity, blend mode and transform. It can also be used as A / B style mixer / switcher

- For pixel accurate cropping / transforming:
	
	- set each layer's 'Fit' parameter to 'Native Resolution'
	
	- set 'Input Filter Type' to 'Nearest Pixel'
	
	- set 'Units' to 'Normalize Coordinates'
	

- 'Swap Layer' not only swaps the source but also all the settings of the layer. ie. move the layer up or down

- 'Swap Layer Source' just swaps the input to the layer and keeps all the settings (blendMode etc..)


- 'A / B Crossfade' slider will set the opacity of any layers that have either A or B selected in their 'Crossfade Group' parameter (crossfade * layer opacity)

- Toggling off 'Enable Transform' and 'Enable Crop' might increase performance if the resolution is set very high or many multiMixs are used in project and are just needed for blending.
